---
title: "Writing & Valorization"
subtitle: "Day 2 - Block 6"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "TI Workshop | D2 B6"
    scrollable: true
    bibliography:
      - ../../../resources/workshop-citations.bib
      - ../../assets/references.bib
    embed-resources: true
---

## Good work first [^s1]

- Programming, writing, thinking
- AI helps across all three
- But you still own quality

```{=html}
<div style="margin-top:0.6em; border:1px dashed #999; padding:0.7em; background:#fafafa; font-size:0.8em;">
<strong>PLACEHOLDER:</strong> add one visual for internal -> core -> broad work<br>
<code>slides/assets/placeholders/d2b6_work_layers.png</code>
</div>
```

## Bring thinking online [^s2]

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  H[Handwritten or tablet notes] --> O[OCR or export]
  O --> M[Markdown or plain text]
  M --> A[AI-assisted structuring]
```

- Slow down before generation
- Keep messy notes first
- Digitize without losing ownership

## Write with AI, not by AI [^s3]

- Pure AI writing is weak
- Generic tone hurts quality
- Human argument still matters

```{=html}
<div style="margin-top:0.6em; border:1px dashed #999; padding:0.7em; background:#fafafa; font-size:0.8em;">
<strong>PLACEHOLDER:</strong> add bad-vs-revised paragraph comparison<br>
<code>slides/assets/placeholders/d2b6_ai_vs_human_paragraph.png</code>
</div>
```

## Use an IDE [^s4]

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  T[Markdown or LaTeX] --> R[Render with Quarto or Pandoc]
  R --> D[docx pdf html slides]
```

- Text files are inspectable
- Version control works naturally
- Render to many formats

## Bullet -> draft -> edit [^s5]

```text
1. Write bullet outline
2. Let AI draft paragraph
3. Edit it yourself
```

- Best simple workflow
- Structure stays human-owned
- Revision improves specificity

## Voice -> paragraph [^s6]

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  V[Voice stream of thought] --> T[Transcript]
  T --> L[LLM cleanup]
  L --> P[Paragraph draft]
  P --> H[Human review]
```

- Dictate when ideas move fast
- Review before reuse
- Useful for rough drafts

```text
Voice: "Main point: firms adopt AI unevenly. We should connect productivity gains to substitution risk and say evidence is still mixed."

AI draft: "AI adoption appears uneven across firms, with productivity gains coexisting alongside substitution risks, while current evidence remains mixed."
```

## Journal policies

- Disclosure rules differ
- Check journal guidance first
- Keep human accountability explicit

```{=html}
<div style="margin-top:0.6em; border:1px dashed #999; padding:0.7em; background:#fafafa; font-size:0.8em;">
<strong>PLACEHOLDER:</strong> add screenshot collage of AI-writing policies<br>
<code>slides/assets/placeholders/d2b6_journal_policies.png</code>
</div>
```

## Feedback -> TODOs [^s7]

```text
Voice note or PDF annotation
-> AI extracts concrete tasks
-> TODO comments in markdown / LaTeX / docx
```

- Feedback becomes actionable
- Keep raw comments attached
- Review before applying edits

## One project, many outputs [^s8]

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  P[Research project] --> W[Website]
  P --> S[Slide deck]
  P --> B[Brief or memo]
  P --> L[LinkedIn post]
  P --> O[Podcast or app]
```

- Same core ideas
- Different audiences
- AI plus code makes this easier

## E7 + E8 handoff [^s9]

- Score two outputs
- Draft adoption memo
- Write personal verification protocol

```{=html}
<div style="font-size:0.65em; opacity:0.85; margin-top:0.8em;">
[^s1]: Sources: @stremersch2024how; @nber:2024; @willisonAgenticPatterns<br>
[^s2]: Sources: @willisonAgenticPatterns; @anthropicContextEngineering2025; @ragSkillMcpRlm<br>
[^s3]: Sources: @blanchardNewToolsNew2025; @linSixFallaciesSubstituting2025<br>
[^s4]: Sources: @opencodeDocs; @claudePlatformDocs; @openaiApiDocs; @willisonAgenticPatterns<br>
[^s5]: Sources: @blanchardNewToolsNew2025; @anthropicContextEngineering2025; @stremersch2024how<br>
[^s6]: Sources: @nber:2024; @stremersch2024how<br>
[^s7]: Sources: @anthropicContextEngineering2025; @blanchardNewToolsNew2025; @ragSkillMcpRlm<br>
[^s8]: Sources: @stremersch2024how; @nber:2024; @opencodeDocs<br>
[^s9]: Sources: @blanchardNewToolsNew2025; @stremersch2024how
</div>
```

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
